title: Edson Serván-Mori
text:
I am an economist and senior health systems researcher at the Centre for Health Systems Research, National Institute of Public Health, Mexico. My work examines how health system design, public financing, institutional fragmentation, and policy volatility shape access to care, financial protection, and health inequalities in Mexico and Latin America.
My research focuses on universal health coverage, social protection in health, out-of-pocket expenditure, catastrophic and impoverishing health spending, health system performance, maternal and child health, non-communicable diseases, and inequalities affecting structurally vulnerable populations. I combine health economics, policy evaluation, applied statistics, quasi-experimental methods, and data science to assess health policies and social protection programmes under real-world institutional constraints.
I have published more than 170 peer-reviewed articles in leading global health, health policy, public health, and health economics journals, including The Lancet, The Lancet Global Health, The Lancet Regional Health – Americas, BMJ Global Health, BMJ Epidemiology and Community Health, Health Policy and Planning, Health Research Policy and Systems, International Journal for Equity in Health, Journal of Global Health, PLOS ONE, and Salud Pública de México. My work also includes books, technical reports, policy analyses, and knowledge-translation outputs for decision-makers.
I have collaborated with national and international organisations including the United Nations Development Programme, the Inter-American Development Bank, the World Bank, the United Nations Population Fund, and academic partners across Mexico, the United States, Europe, and Latin America. I am a Senior Collaborator of the Global Burden of Disease Study and participate in international research networks focused on health equity, social epidemiology, and health system fragmentation.
I am a member of Mexico’s National System of Researchers, Level II, recipient of the 2015 INSP Young Investigator Award, and serve on the editorial boards of PLOS ONE, BMC Health Services Research, Health Research Policy and Systems, Health Policy Open, and Frontiers in Public Health. I previously served on the editorial board of The International Journal of Health Planning and Management.
I hold a PhD in Economics from the National Autonomous University of Mexico, an MSc in Economics from CIDE, and a BA in Economics from Universidad Nacional Agraria La Molina, Peru, with additional training in applied statistics, data science, and social development.
